title: silene vulgaris subsp. tenoreana
intro: Silene vulgaris subsp. tenoreana, also known as Tenore's catchfly, is a subspecies of the white campion plant that belongs to the carnation family. It is a perennial herb with medicinal properties, primarily due to its rich content of saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, thanks to its expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. Silene vulgaris subsp. tenoreana can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional folk medicine to promote digestion, reduce stress, and boost the immune system.
